summary: authcascade fetches and evals remote code at require time and stages host data for exfiltration
details: On require('authcascade'), lib/writer.js (loaded transitively via the package main pino.js) issues an HTTP GET to a base64-decoded jsonkeeper.com paste URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PJNZP) and passes the response body directly to eval(). A second hex-obfuscated jsonkeeper paste URL (.../b/HY6M6) is staged in the same module. The same scope assembles a payload object containing the full process.env, os.platform(), os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and non-internal MAC addresses, which is reachable to the eval'd remote code — a classic remote-controlled credential/host-fingerprint exfiltration channel. The package additionally misrepresents itself: package.json describes an authentication-token library while the tarball ships the pino logger codebase (pino.js entry, lib/proto.js, pino-banner.png, pino branding in README) with the malicious lib/writer.js grafted on. The jsonkeeper.com paste content is attacker-mutable, so any installer importing this package executes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with full access to environment variables and host identifiers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
